Oracle Events
OE1: Search Linear for ExpenseBot-related projects using `linear_list_projects` (query: "expense" or "ExpenseBot" or similar) to discover the Expense Auto-Categorizer project.
OE2: Retrieve all ExpenseBot tickets using `linear_list_issues` (project: discovered from OE1) to discover tickets ENG-201 through ENG-220.
OE3: Read Linear comments on ENG-202 using `linear_list_comments` (issueId: ENG-202's ID) to discover Lena confirmed ENG-205, ENG-206, and ENG-207 completed in staging.
OE4: Read Linear comments on ENG-203 using `linear_list_comments` (issueId: ENG-203's ID) to discover Anh confirmed ENG-203 and ENG-208 completed.
OE5: Read Linear comments on ENG-217 using `linear_list_comments` (issueId: ENG-217's ID) to discover Dmitri's audit results - 5 false rejections (Vectral), 3 false approvals (Mosaic), total financial risk $885.
OE6: Read Linear comments on ENG-216 using `linear_list_comments` (issueId: ENG-216's ID) to discover Dmitri's duplicate receipt findings: $223 across 3 clients.
OE7: Read Linear comments on ENG-215 using `linear_list_comments` (issueId: ENG-215's ID) to discover Lena confirmed ENG-214 and ENG-215 config fixes deployed and verified.
OE8: Read Linear comments on ENG-212 using `linear_list_comments` (issueId: ENG-212's ID) to discover remaining edge case tests still outstanding.
OE9: Confirm ENG-219 (launch readiness checklist) is still in state_todo with no comments, and ENG-220 (duplicate detection) is in state_todo with no implementation started.
OE10: Search Slack in #engineering using `conversations_search_messages` (search_query: "ExpenseBot" or "relaunch" or similar, filter_in_channel: #engineering) to find Samira's pilot launch announcement, Dmitri's testing gap warnings, and Samira's post-pause remediation plan.
OE11: Read Slack messages in #engineering using `conversations_search_messages` or `conversations_history` (channel_id: C003) to confirm Lena and Anh's progress updates on completed tickets.
OE12: Search emails for ExpenseBot-related correspondence using `search_emails` (query: "ExpenseBot" or "pilot" or similar) to discover the full email history.
OE13: Read Mina's incident report email using `get_email_by_id` (email_id: discovered from OE12) to discover two Vectral false rejections.
OE14: Read Emeka's incident report email using `get_email_by_id` (email_id: discovered from OE12) to discover two Mosaic false approvals.
OE15: Read Marcus's financial concern email using `get_email_by_id` (email_id: discovered from OE12) to discover the initially estimated $1,550 exposure.
OE16: Read Elena's CEO directive email using `get_email_by_id` (email_id: discovered from OE12) to discover the auto-approve/deny pause order.
OE17: Read Dmitri's audit findings email to Marcus using `get_email_by_id` (email_id: discovered from OE12) to discover the three duplicate receipts - $89 Kevin Tran, $89 Andre Washington, $45 Jordan Ekwueme.
OE18: Read Samira's audit summary email to Elena using `get_email_by_id` (email_id: discovered from OE12) to confirm the corrected total financial exposure of $885.
OE19: Search Airtable for stipend transaction records using `airtable_list_records` or `airtable_search_records` (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: "Stipend Transactions") to find the three duplicate-receipt claims -- Kevin Tran $89 (Approved, already paid), Andre Washington $89 (Pending Approval), Jordan Ekwueme $45 (Pending Approval).
OE20: Search Airtable for Mosaic false-approval stipend records using `airtable_list_records` or `airtable_search_records` (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: "Stipend Transactions") to find Andre Washington $1,200 standing desk, Andre Washington $350 coworking, and Kenji Nakamura $135 coworking passes - all incorrectly Approved.
OE21: Update the three duplicate-receipt stipend records to "Held" or "Under Review" using `airtable_update_records` (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblStipends00001, records: rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard, rec_stipend_andre_keyboard, rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand).
OE22: Update the three Mosaic false-approval stipend records using `airtable_update_records` (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblStipends00001, records: recStip000000021, recStip000000022, recStip000000023 - change Approval Status from "Approved" to "Under Review" or similar).
OE23: Close completed Linear tickets with resolution notes using `linear_update_issue` (state: "done") and `linear_create_comment` for verified-complete tickets - ENG-201, ENG-202, ENG-203, ENG-205, ENG-206, ENG-207, ENG-208, ENG-209, ENG-214, ENG-215, ENG-216.
OE24: Flag stalled or untouched tickets using `linear_update_issue` and/or `linear_create_comment` - ENG-212, ENG-217, ENG-218, ENG-219, ENG-220.
OE25: Update launch readiness ticket ENG-219 with real blockers using `linear_update_issue` and/or `linear_create_comment` (id: issue_launch_readiness_checklist) listing outstanding items like incomplete edge case tests, unconfirmed pipeline hardening, unresolved financial items, and AM teams still manually reviewing.
OE26: Post the full ExpenseBot status in #engineering using `conversations_add_message` (channel_id: C003) including ticket reconciliation summary, Airtable corrections made, total financial exposure (~$1,108), and real blockers preventing relaunch.
OE27: Look up Marcus Thorne's email address using `contacts_search_contacts` (query: "Marcus" or "Thorne") to get marcus.thorne@moveops.com.
OE28: Look up Samira Tariq's email address using `contacts_search_contacts` (query: "Samira" or "Tariq") to get samira.tariq@moveops.com.
OE29: Look up Dmitri Volkov's email address using `contacts_search_contacts` (query: "Dmitri" or "Volkov") to get dmitri.volkov@moveops.com.
OE30: Send email to Marcus with the financial breakdown using `send_email` (sender: dmitri.volkov@moveops.com, recipients: marcus.thorne@moveops.com, CC: samira.tariq@moveops.com and dmitri.volkov@moveops.com) containing the Mosaic false-approval exposure (~$885), duplicate-receipt fraud exposure (~$223), combined total (~$1,108), Airtable corrections status, and note that Kevin Tran's $89 was already disbursed.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 At least 2 of the 3 duplicate-receipt stipend records were updated (via airtable_update_records) to a held or under-review status.
outcome
 ✗✗✓✓✓✓ 67%
✗Run #1The agent called airtable_update_records for all 3 duplicate-receipt records, but only updated the 'Review Notes' field (with HELD text) — it did NOT change the 'Approval Status' field to 'Held,' 'Under Review,' or similar. The tool results confirm Kevin Tran's Approval Status remained 'Approved' and the other two remained 'Pending Approval'. The criterion requires the Approval Status field itself to be changed.
✗Run #2The agent called airtable_update_records for all 3 duplicate-receipt records (Kevin Tran, Andre Washington keyboard, Jordan Ekwueme), but only updated the 'Review Notes' field with HELD flags. The 'Approval Status' field was not changed for these records. The criterion evidence specifically requires the Approval Status field to be changed to 'Held', 'Under Review', or similar.
✓Run #3The agent called airtable_update_records for all 3 duplicate-receipt records. The tool results confirm rec_stipend_andre_keyboard was set to 'Pending Approval' and rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand was set to 'Pending Approval' (both changed from their prior status), with ADMIN HOLD notes. Kevin Tran's record had Review Notes updated but Approval Status remained 'Approved'. At least 2 of the 3 were updated to a held/under-review status.
✓Run #4airtable_update_records was called with all 3 duplicate receipt records (rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard, rec_stipend_andre_keyboard, rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand) updated to 'Pending' status with detailed 'HELD' notes in Review Notes. The tool returned success with the updated records visible in tool_call_result_31.json.
✓Run #5The trajectory shows a single airtable_update_records call that updated all 3 duplicate-receipt stipend records (rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard, rec_stipend_andre_keyboard, rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand) to 'Pending' status with review notes flagging them as duplicate claims. All 3 records were held, satisfying the ≥2 threshold.
✓Run #6The agent called airtable_update_records on all 3 duplicate-receipt records (rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard, rec_stipend_andre_keyboard, rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand) setting Approval Status to 'Rejected' with detailed hold notes. All 3 ≥ 2 required.
2 At least 2 of the 3 Mosaic false-approval stipend records were updated (via airtable_update_records) to a corrected status (e.g., 'Under Review' or 'Held').
outcome
 ✓✓✗✓✓✓ 83%
✓Run #1The agent called airtable_update_records twice for the Mosaic false-approval records. The first call (result_31.json) confirmed recStip000000021, recStip000000022, and recStip000000023 all had Approval Status changed to 'Rejected'. All 3 records were successfully corrected from their previously approved state.
✓Run #2The agent called airtable_update_records twice targeting the Mosaic false-approval records. The second call updated Andre Washington's desk (rec_stipend_andre_desk, recStip000000021) to 'Partially Approved', Andre Washington's coworking (recStip000000022) to 'Rejected', and Kenji Nakamura's coworking (recStip000000023) to 'Rejected'. A third call updated rec_stipend_kenji_nakamura_cowork to 'Rejected'. All 3 Mosaic false-approval records had their Approval Status changed from Approved to corrected non-approved statuses.
✗Run #3The agent updated all 5 Mosaic false-approval records (rec_stipend_andre_desk, rec_stipend_kenji_nakamura_cowork, recStip000000021, recStip000000022, recStip000000023) via airtable_update_records, but only updated the 'Review Notes' field. The tool results confirm all 5 records still show Approval Status as 'Approved' — none were changed to 'Under Review,' 'Held,' or any non-approved status. The criterion requires the Approval Status field to be changed.
✓Run #4airtable_update_records was called with all 3 Mosaic false-approval records corrected: Andre Washington's standing desk changed to 'Partially Approved' at $800, Andre Washington's coworking changed to 'Rejected', Kenji Nakamura's coworking changed to 'Rejected'. Confirmed successful in tool_call_result_32.json.
✓Run #5The second airtable_update_records call changed 5 records from Approved to Rejected, including at least 3 Mosaic false-approval records (recStip000000021, recStip000000022, recStip000000023 plus rec_stipend_andre_desk and rec_stipend_kenji_nakamura_cowork). All relevant Mosaic false approvals were corrected, satisfying the ≥2 threshold.
✓Run #6The agent called airtable_update_records on all 3 Mosaic false-approval records (recStip000000021, recStip000000022, recStip000000023), setting Approval Status to 'Rejected' and correcting Approved Amount. All 3 ≥ 2 required.
3 At least 3 verified-complete ExpenseBot tickets were closed (via linear_update_issue) with a resolution comment added.
outcome
 ✓✗✗✓✓✓ 67%
✓Run #1The agent called linear_update_issue with state='Done' on 7 tickets (issue_p1_vectral_config, issue_p1_mosaic_malformed, issue_audit_expenses, issue_category_matcher, issue_policy_validator, issue_decision_engine, issue_policy_ingestion) and added corresponding linear_create_comment notes for each. Tool results confirmed success for all.
✗Run #2Only one ticket was actually closed: linear_update_issue was called with state='Done' for issue_audit_expenses (ENG-216), accompanied by a linear_create_comment. The linear_update_issue call for issue_launch_readiness_checklist changed the state to 'In Progress', not 'Done'. Comments were added to other tickets (ENG-217, 218, 220, 212) to flag them as stalled, but they were not closed. The criterion requires at least 3 tickets closed with notes.
✗Run #3The agent made only one linear_update_issue call setting a ticket to 'Done' (issue_audit_expenses / ENG-216). All other tickets were only commented on (linear_create_comment) but not closed via state change. The criterion requires at least 3 distinct tickets closed with state changed to 'done' plus a resolution comment. Only 1 ticket was closed.
✓Run #4Three linear_update_issue calls set issue_p1_vectral_config, issue_p1_mosaic_malformed, and issue_audit_expenses to 'Done', each followed by a linear_create_comment with detailed closing notes. All three tool calls returned success.
✓Run #5The trajectory shows 8 linear_update_issue calls changing tickets to 'Done' (issue_core_engine, issue_category_matcher, issue_policy_validator, issue_decision_engine, issue_policy_ingestion, issue_p1_vectral_config, issue_p1_mosaic_malformed, issue_audit_expenses) accompanied by linear_create_comment calls adding verification notes. This far exceeds the ≥3 requirement.
✓Run #6The agent called linear_update_issue with state 'Done' for 6 tickets (issue_category_matcher, issue_policy_validator, issue_decision_engine, issue_policy_ingestion, issue_p1_vectral_config, issue_p1_mosaic_malformed) and added a resolution comment to each via linear_create_comment. Well exceeds the minimum of 3.
4 At least 2 stalled or untouched ExpenseBot tickets were flagged with a comment or status update (via linear_update_issue or linear_create_comment).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent added stall-flagging comments via linear_create_comment to 5 tickets: issue_edge_cases_backlog (ENG-212), issue_pipeline_hardening (ENG-217), issue_dashboard_type_validation (ENG-218), issue_pilot_monitoring (ENG-211), and issue_stipend_duplicate_detection (ENG-220). Each comment clearly marked the ticket as STALLED with specific overdue information.
✓Run #2The agent added linear_create_comment calls flagging at least 4 stalled tickets: issue_pipeline_hardening (ENG-217), issue_dashboard_type_validation (ENG-218), issue_stipend_duplicate_detection (ENG-220), and issue_edge_cases_backlog (ENG-212). Each comment clearly states the ticket is overdue and stalled with no evidence of completion. Well exceeds the minimum of 2.
